ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-30-2011 IN DEPT. 220, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE. ARGUED AND THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AND MODIFIED AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D). THE TRIAL IS SET FOR JULY 11, 2011 AT 11:15AM IN DEPT. 220 AND MSC WILL BE HELD IN ROOM 603 ON JUNE 23, 2011 AT 1:00PM. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR TRIAL PREFERENCE IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITIONS THAT: 1) MR. BRECKLER'S DEPOSITION BE COMPLETED NO LATER THAN APRIL 15, 2011 AND 2) PLAINTIFFS AGREE THAT MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ADJUDICATION MAY BE FILED ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS JUNE 23, 2011. ALL EXPERT DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL DEPARTMENT. THE CLOSING OF PERCIPIENT DISCOVERY IS 7/11/11. PLAINTIFFS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 5/10/11. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL RELY ON PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR REBUTTAL OF PLAINTIFFS ELEMENTS THAT THEY NEED TO PROVE AS WELL AS ANYTHING THAT DEFENDANTS NEED TO PROVE, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES RELY, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 5/17/11. PARTIES SHALL EXCHANGE A PARED DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL. PLAINTIFFS MUST MAKE A DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN JUNE 16, 2011. PLAINTIFFS SHALL PROVIDE COPIES OF ANY MEDICARE LIENS OR BANKRUPTCY TRUST CLAIM FORMS TO BERRY & BERRY BY 5/13/11 AND ANY SUBSEQUENT FILINGS SHALL BE DISCLOSED WITHIN 3 COURT DAYS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (220)

MOTION To Compel Deft Rockwell Automation, Inc. To Compensate Dr. Richard Cohen, M.D. His Ordinary And Reasonable Fees; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Rodrick Breckler & Joann Breckler
Nicole E. Gage, Esq., of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2105), appearing for defendant / opposing party Rockwell Automation. Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS RODRICK BRECKLER, JOANN BRECKLER'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC. TO COMPENSATE DR. RICHARD COHEN, M.D. HIS ORDINARY AND REASONABLE FEES; AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to compel Rockwell Automation, Inc. to compensate Dr. Richard Cohen his ordinary and reasonable fees and request for sanctions is denied. The deposition was noticed for and understood by Dr. Cohen's counsel as seeking only historical information about Dr. Cohen's work at Varian and not as an expert, or as a provider of current medical information. Neither Dr. Cohen nor his counsel conditioned the deposition going forward on payment of an "expert fee" nor was there any court order or stipulation in advance of the deposition providing for payment of any fee to Dr. Cohen other than the standard witness fee. Under these circumstances, and notwithstanding the statement of Dr. Cohen's counsel at the outset of the deposition that Dr. Cohen will be seeking an expert fee, and in the absence of any authority providing for payment of such a fee, no expert fee need be paid to Dr. Cohen. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT R.E. CUDDIE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant R.E. Cuddie Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence, loss of consortium and premises owner/contractor liability, granted as to strict liability and off calendar as to false representation and punitive damages. As to negligence, loss of consortium and premises owner/contractor liability, Mr. Breckler's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing tiles removed and installed by defendant. As to strict liability, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Breckler was exposed to an asbestos-containing product manufactured, sold, supplied or distributed by defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they will dismiss their claims for false representation and punitive damages. Defendant's objections to Mr. Breckler's declaration are overruled. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR # 11288 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT PACIFIC SCIENTIFIC COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Pacific Scientific Company's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Breckler was exposed to asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. By not including plaintiffs' responses to contention discovery, defendant is unable to successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The other evidence submitted by defendant is insufficient to satisfy the negate an element prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs' objection to the declaration of Ms. Stravalle-Schmidt is sustained. Order signed in open court. The Court further ordered a hearing on defendant's exparte application for motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents. An order regarding the exparte application was signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR # 11288 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CHESTER C. LEHMANN CO., INC. DBA ELECTRICAL DISTRIBUTORS CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Anne T. Acuna, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; Erik P. Khoobyarian, Esq. & Tina Naicker, Esq. of Hopkins & Carley 408/286-9800 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Chester C. Lehmann Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and loss of consortium and off calendar as to false representation. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not posses and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Breckler was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant did not include in its moving papers plaintiffs' responses to all of its special interrogatories, including the interrogatories asking for persons with knowledge of the facts supporting plaintiffs' claims, thus defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses. Further, Mr. Breckler's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Breckler's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing "asbestos wire" attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they will dismiss their false representation cause of action. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(220/HEK)

Plaintiffs concede the lack of merit to their claims for strict liability and false representation and their request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court.
